cards.back: Matplotlib is a plotting library for the Python programming language, providing a comprehensive set of tools for creating high-quality 2D and 3D plots.
Seaborn is a Python data visualization library based on Matplotlib. It provides a high-level interface for drawing attractive and informative statistical graphics.

cards.front: What is the purpose of Line Plots in Matplotlib?
cards.back: Line plots are used to display the relationship between two variables, with each point on the plot representing a data point. They are commonly used for time series data.

cards.front: What is the purpose of Scatter Plots in Matplotlib?
cards.back: Scatter plots are used to display the relationship between two variables, with each point on the plot representing a data point. They are commonly used to identify clusters or patterns in the data.

cards.front: What is the difference between Matplotlib and Seaborn?
cards.back: Matplotlib is a lower-level library that provides a wide range of visualization tools, while Seaborn is a higher-level library that provides a simplified interface for creating attractive statistical graphics.
